# JOS-S07: All Governed Components Must Be Registered

> **Renamed 2026-05-20 (per JOS-D0077):** previously titled "All Governed
> Objects Must Be Registered." Legacy filename `JOS-S07-all-governed-objects-registered.md`
> retained during 90-day SOP-014 coexistence window (ends 2026-08-20);
> rename to `JOS-S07-all-governed-components-registered.md` deferred to
> the Phase 2 rename batch per JOS-D0077.

## Rule

Every governed component in a JOS-adopting repository MUST appear in that
repository's **Component Registry** at `.jos/registry.yaml` (per [JOS-D0077] +
[JOS-SPEC-021]). "Governed component" means every component in either of
the two governance layers defined by JOS-D0039:

### Content Layer
- Concepts
- Principles
- Standards
- Rules
- Contracts
- Decisions
- Standing Orders

### Delivery Layer
- SOPs (standard operating procedures)
- Playbooks (guidance frameworks)
- Gates (blocking checkpoints)
- Templates (scaffolds)
- Monitors (automated surveillance)
- Specs (normative definitions)

Templates are included, even though they do not carry individual UUIDs —
the template set as a whole is a governed artifact.

## Rationale

JOS-SO-005 (Registry Is Structural Truth) and JOS-R02 (Registry Entry
Required) were written when JOS had only content-layer objects. Phase 5
introduced the delivery layer (JOS-D0039). Without an explicit standard,
it is ambiguous whether "registry entry required" extends to delivery
instruments — and in practice, Phase 5 shipped with instruments missing
from the registry. This standard removes the ambiguity.

## Enforcement

- **Gate:** Registry-Filesystem Sync (`JOS-GATE-002`) — verifies every
  governed file has a matching registry entry and vice versa.
- **Review:** Charter-sync operations must include registry entries for
  any new delivery instruments distributed to governed repos.

## Exceptions

None. This is a `required` standard. Objects that are not intended to be
governed (e.g., personal notes in `WORKBENCH/`, unstructured reference
data in `libraries/`) are not subject to this standard because they are
not governed components in the first place.

## Related

- `JOS-SO-005` — Registry Is Structural Truth (the parent standing order)
- `JOS-R02` — Registry Entry Required (the content-layer rule this extends)
- `JOS-D0039` — Governance Has Two Layers: Content and Delivery (the
  decision this operationalizes)
- `JOS-GATE-002` — Registry-Filesystem Sync Gate (the enforcement mechanism)
- **`JOS-D0077`** — Components-as-Parts-on-a-Shelf (defines "component" as
  the canonical word; ratifies the Component Registry as the single
  source of truth)
- **`JOS-C49`** — Components as Parts on a Shelf (concept)
- **`JOS-SPEC-021`** — Registry / Index / Manifest Schema (strict no-mix
  rules for the registry vs sub-views)
